title: Hour Bank & Pay Premiums Calculator
meta_title: Hour Bank, Hazard Pay & Night Shift Calculator | CalcForgeTools
meta_description: Convert hour bank balances to cash value and accurately calculate hazardous condition bonuses and night shift premiums according to Brazilian labor laws.

hero.title: Hour Bank & Pay Premiums
hero.subtitle: Determine exactly how much your accumulated overtime bank is worth and calculate mandatory 30% hazard or health-risk premiums.

formula: Gross Pay = Base + Risk Premium + (Hourly Rate x 20% x Night Hours)
formula_explanation: Under CLT law, health-risk premiums are calculated using the national minimum wage (R$ 1,621 in 2026), whereas physical-hazard premiums are 30% of your exact base salary. Night shifts command a mandatory 20% bump per hour.
example: If your base is R$ 2,500 and you operate under hazardous conditions (periculosidade), your salary jumps by a flat R$ 750, altering the weight of every hour accumulated.

faq.question: Can I receive both Hazard and Unhealthy condition premiums?
faq.answer: No. Brazilian labor law expressly forbids accumulating them. If exposed to both, the employee will automatically be granted whichever yields the highest monetary payout.

faq.question: What is the basis for the Unhealthy Premium?
faq.answer: Supreme Court rulings stipulate it uses the National Minimum Wage irrespective of how much you actually earn. It applies at 10%, 20%, or 40% brackets.

faq.question: How do Hour Banks translate to money?
faq.answer: If hour banks aren't depleted via guaranteed time-off within the legal timeframe (usually 6 to 12 months), the company must liquidate the balance to cash. Our tool calculates the base hourly worth for conversion.

related_guides.title: Understanding Brazilian Pay Premiums
related_guides.description: Discover how and when premiums apply legally.
